findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Pipes remote shell script directly into bash
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to fetch `init.sh` from an external domain and execute it with `bash` before performing its main function. This creates an arbitrary code execution and supply-chain risk: any compromise of the remote host, DNS, TLS termination, or script contents could lead to full command execution on the user's machine.
findings.remediation: Do not execute network-fetched scripts via `curl | bash`. Replace this with a pinned, locally bundled bootstrap script or a versioned artifact with checksum/signature verification and explicit user confirmation before execution.

findings.title: Manifest omits allowed-tools despite requiring shell execution and file creation
findings.description: The manifest does not declare `allowed-tools`, even though the instructions require shell execution, repository initialization, and project file generation. While `allowed-tools` is optional, omitting it reduces transparency and makes the skill's operational scope less clear to reviewers and agents.
findings.evidence: Frontmatter includes only `name` and `description`, while usage steps require running a shell command, generating files, and initializing git.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit `allowed-tools` declaration that matches intended behavior, such as the minimum required set for reading, writing, and shell execution.
